note: Entirely wheelchair/scooter accessible via the Atrium entrance with lifts, accessible toilets every floor, free loaner chairs/rollators and free companion entry. Rembrandt's Night Watch. A model of accessibility.
Fully accessible with lifts and ramps, free loaner wheelchairs (reserve ahead), free companion ticket + audio guide, accessible toilets. Priority entry for disabled visitors.

The modern annex/museum areas are accessible, but Anne Frank's actual hidden loft is reachable ONLY by steep stairs with no lift - not accessible to wheelchair users. Virtual tour offered for inaccessible parts.

A must-do and a great way to rest your arms/battery - but accessibility varies by operator: Blue Boat and Lovers offer wheelchair-accessible boats with adapted boarding (book ahead). Many small open-boat tours have 3 steps to board and are NOT accessible.
Amsterdam's largest park with flat paved paths throughout, benches, cafes and open-air theatre in summer; accessible toilets near main entrances. Easy and lovely for families.
Flat public square between the Rijks, Van Gogh and Stedelijk museums, crossed by accessible paths; the classic photo spot. Easy hub between the big museums.
Popular interactive brewery attraction; generally wheelchair accessible with lifts. Fun, indoor, good rainy-day option.
Picturesque canal-side neighbourhood; mostly flat but with patches of cobbles, narrow pavements and canal-bridge slopes. Charming, navigable with care.
Famous floating flower market on the Singel; largely flat and reachable, though stalls get crowded. A quick colourful stop.
Historic city-centre zoo; largely accessible paved paths, popular with families. Confirm specific building access on arrival.
